storyInfo: The hatred from losing his dearest friend surges through Xanthos's heart, with nowhere to go. The old man reveals a grimy past, allowing Xanthos to determine the true reason the Padishah sent a delegation to exchange relics.

attributes.content: You really sure about leaving the village?
attributes.name: Concerned Villager

attributes.content: I'll be off at first light tomorrow.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: The fields have already been replowed, there's plenty of seeds and grain, and the storehouse has also been fixed. As for the ferrying, I've taught a few of the young ones. Don't worry.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I'm worried about you, Xanthos.
attributes.name: Concerned Villager

attributes.content: You've never left the marsh before, and now you want to run off into the distance, all to find a stranger?
attributes.name: Concerned Villager

attributes.content: Ithakos is my brother. He's helped us all out before.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It's been an entire year. Maybe he's already headed back to Athenius... That's where he was from, it makes sense.
attributes.name: Concerned Villager

attributes.content: The things he agreed to do, he will do.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Once the war is over, he will return. He promised me.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Have you ever considered... if... if he's already...
attributes.name: Concerned Villager

attributes.content: He went off... to fight the Sargonians...
attributes.name: Concerned Villager

attributes.content: Then I shall avenge him. I shall avenge the Minoans.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: <p=1>Northern Mountains, Minos</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: Hey, don't get so worked up! You've got the wrong guy! I'm really not your Ithakos or whatever it was!
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: The one getting worked up is you, friend. I merely recognized the armor you wear.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It belonged to Ithakos, my brother. Before he left, every family in our village donated leather and iron for it. I was the one who made that set of armor presentable, and forged him his weapon.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: F-Forgive me, I've been battling for so long now, big battles, small ones, and it's been a long time since I had some proper sleep. My mind's...
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: Here. There's still some calming herbal tea in the pot.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Can you tell me who you got this armor from?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Another guerrilla friend. We ran into a skirmish... He took an arrow, and before he died, he gave it to me...
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: But his name wasn't Ithakos.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: Phew...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You see all these marks on the armor? It must've passed through a lot of hands to get that many...
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: You might not know this, but we guerrillas aren't like the Sargonian army. We're not well-supplied.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: Everyone gathers up weapons, armor and food, which get given out depending on the mission's priority.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: I can tell you that fallen guerrilla's unit number, and that'll get you where you need to be. Oh, right, let me give you the armor back first.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: Keep it. It's still of use to you. Just telling me the way is enough, friend.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: If you insist, but are you sure you want to search the mountains?
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: These mountains are our stomping grounds, and where we battle the Sargonians...
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: Then I'll join you.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You ever seen a battlefield before?
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member A

attributes.content: (Shakes head) Before all this, I had only ever held oars and farm tools.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: But we have no other choice, do we?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Sorry, I don't really remember the smiling, long-haired Forte you mentioned.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member B

attributes.content: As for these boots, I got given them when I joined the guerrillas... According to the friend who gave them to me, they belonged to a middle-aged guy who was missing a chunk out of his left horn.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member B

attributes.content: There's a hole in them.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: S-Sorry. They're a little small for me, and I haven't had the time to mend them.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member B

attributes.content: Those Sargonians have been chasing us this entire time. If you hadn't shown up to support us in the nick of time...
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member B

attributes.content: Sit down.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Huh?
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member B

attributes.content: I'll help you mend them.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Na'sai kala... Wow, you've got big hands, but they're nimble... How long've you been one of us?
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member B

attributes.content: Four months. I came here to find someone, at first.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Keep the boots.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Fighting, marching, hiding, reorganizing. You bleed, you die, or you wipe your sword, then you jump into the next battle... Days like these are long ones.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Where's the man who gave you these boots? Just point me the way, friend.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I knew this letter carved on the corner of this shield meant something... You're saying it's short for 'Ithakos', and you carved it?
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member C

attributes.content: (Nods)
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: So you've spent half a year wandering the battlefields, staying with pretty much every one of our bands, just to find this guy?
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member C

attributes.content: It seems you don't have much you can tell me either.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Sorry, Xanthos. Also, I don't plan on giving this shield back to you.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member C

attributes.content: Look at just how many blade marks there are on it! And these black marks... Sargonian Arts can barely make a dent in it!
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member C

attributes.content: I need to keep it. It'll let me protect more of our friends, maybe even keep me alive long enough to go home one day.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member C

attributes.content: If that is the case, then I couldn't be happier.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Let the name 'Ithakos' stay by your side. Let our blood, our eyes, be witness to our victory.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: This is where we part, my friend.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member C

attributes.content: Just point me the way. It is all I ask.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Try changing hands. Ithakos was left-handed, which I kept in mind when I made this Arts unit for him.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I knew it! I knew something felt off about the power levels!
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member D

attributes.content: ...It's already been a whole year, Xanthos.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member D

attributes.content: Time flies, as swift as a melted river.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You're already the most famous guerrilla in the mountains. You've fought the most fights, walked the most battlefields. Everybody sings of your heroism...
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member D

attributes.content: Are you really going to keep searching for your brother?
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member D

attributes.content: I have no reason to stop.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Like how you will not turn and leave the battlefield.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Hah, you're right.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member D

attributes.content: Your accent. You're from Lachedamon?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I sure am. Well, more specifically, my hometown is on the route the nomadic city follows. It's a river valley, absolutely beautiful in spring.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member D

attributes.content: I've just told you about my hometown, the village Ithakos and I founded together. You should tell me more about yours.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: We have been losing this war for over a hundred years. We do not know how it will end. We should remember each other's hometowns... We should remember each other.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I'll be more than happy to, my friend.
attributes.name: Guerrilla Squad Member D

attributes.content: I continue my search tomorrow. Just point me the way, friend.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Why must I suffer the indignity of being tied to this tree, Minoan?
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: Like I said, answer my question.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Ithakos, Ithakos, Ithakos! Always about Ithakos!
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: Fine, fine, tozz feek, I've had enough... Two years ago, my troops met with Minoan guerrillas near here. They were escorting some wounded.
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: I remember their ensign, matches the unit you mentioned.
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: We wiped them out. That Ithakos you keep talking about should've been one of them.
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: You happy now? Did you seriously set up an ambush for the sake of a battle two years ago, so you could capture me and have your revenge?
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: You're mad, Minoan, an absolute lunatic!
attributes.name: Sargonian Officer

attributes.content: You might not believe me, but this is all just a coincidence.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Before today, I was not thinking of finding Ithakos, or killing his enemies.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: This battle is no different to the ones of the past year, no, the past century.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You invaded land that doesn't belong to you, Sargonian, and we have no choice but to defend our home.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I have simply become accustomed to asking everyone I meet: 'Have you seen Ithakos? Have you seen that smiling, long-haired Forte? He's from Athenius, but he has a south country accent.'
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Such a thing truly does mean I am, as you say, 'mad'.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I have found his armor, his boots, his shield, his Arts unit... Everything he left behind on this wooded mountain, I have found.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I have found him.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I have never once thought you invaders would remember the name of every Minoan you met. Your padishahs hardly care about what every line in your books of merit actually means, do they?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: That said... Ithakos. Remember this name.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It is our passion, our love, and your crime, your punishment.
attributes.name: Xanthos
Xanthos
attributes.content: Now, tell me.

attributes.name: Xanthos
attributes.content: Where was that battle, exactly?!

attributes.name: Xanthos
attributes.content: Which of these firs was watered with the blood of Minos?!

attributes.text: Xanthos looks up, and sees countless Minoan firs, standing tall. The wind blows, and the entire forest rustles.
This land, these woods. They remember many things, but they do not speak.
His brother, his friend, the youth who followed his revelation and left home. He died here, somewhere. Everywhere.
Ithakos, countless Ithakoses, have left not even bones, all for the sake of the land beneath their feet.
Xanthos lifts the giant oar in his hand. After an entire year, it has forgotten the river's burble. Now, all that can wet it is the blood of his enemy!

attributes.content: Did you avenge Ithakos?
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Just as I was about to smash in the head of that Sargonian with my weapon, my friend stopped me, while the bugles cried out.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You never told me the campaign you experienced was...
attributes.name: Lykeion
Xanthos
attributes.content: The final battle before Minos and Sargon signed a ceasefire. Minos, which had lost for a hundred years,

attributes.name: Xanthos
attributes.content: hah, had finally won.
That friend of mine desperately held back my hand. He had a broken leg, yet he could still bring such strength to bear to rein me in.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I turned, and saw his face full of tears, and his eyes full of fear... Not towards the Sargonians, but towards me.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I knew what he was so afraid of.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: He was afraid I would destroy this hard-won 'peace'. He was afraid I would bring about new conflict, that I would extend this cruel war. He was afraid he would become like Ithakos, forever unable to return home.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: My pent-up rage and grief had nowhere to go. All I could do was watch that Sargonian officer kneel on the ground, numbly listening to the bugles. The same numbness I felt...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It was that moment I knew the war will rage on in our bodies. It had begun, and it could never end.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You are right. The Minos in my heart no longer exists. It died on the battlefield many years ago, alongside the Ithakoses.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: But I will not allow the enemy to defile our land again. Not even if they had built this city.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: 'Not even if they had built this city'... So, even the textbooks say that nowadays?
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Ithakos went to your village, so full of passion, unlike me. He must have taught you many building techniques.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: Indeed. He renovated nearly every important building in the village.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: His techniques were passed down from his ancestors. During the occupation, my own grandfather was pressed into service by the Sargonians to build the nomadic cities, thanks to his mastery of engineering.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: The mobility layer of this Athenius might just be his handiwork.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: Forgive me, I was far too rude to you earlier. I...
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Do not bow your head for that, boy.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: I am not the engineer who built the nomadic cities, I don't have proof of how much our ancestors toiled building Athenius. All that data was taken away by the Sargonians.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: The Sargonians wanted the Minoan engineers' data... but why?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: An engineer's notes will always have something more than just jargon and modification blueprints.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: He had to keep track of the quantities of building materials, while coordinating transport and construction teams, so he was a meticulous planner of manpower, and remembered the names of every worker.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: Not Sargonian workers, but the names of those from Minos, and where they came from, and how the Sargonians forced them to do their bidding.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father
Lykeion
attributes.content: Minoan workers? As I recall, current mainstream thinking is that Padishah Malla did not use any Minoan workers for heavy labor while the Three Poleis were being built.

attributes.name: Lykeion
attributes.content: Do you mean to say...?
History was not written by us, not even our final victory.
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: But there is evidence of wrongdoing, evidence that should be shared with the public. Do you remember where these engineer's notes are?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: How should I know? Ask the Sargonians... Ah, but those Sargonians are probably dead by now, haha...
attributes.name: Ithakos's Father

attributes.content: Ask the Sargonians... The construction of the Three Poleis...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: No!
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: What is it, Master?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: The Sargonian mission is not using the exchange to obtain technical data, but rather, the notes detailing proof of the Sargonians' sins!
attributes.name: Xanthos
